title: alstroemeria magnifica subsp. gayana
intro: Alstroemeria magnifica subsp. gayana, also known as the Peruvian Lily or Parrot's Beak, is a flowering plant species belonging to the Alstroemeria genus. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. Consuming Alstroemeria magnifica subsp. gayana is said to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions.
